summary: DHH announces the launch of the new Ruby on Rails website and official logo, designed by John Hicks and commissioned by the community. The relaunch consolidates multiple Rails web properties into one unified presence, including manuals, a Trac system for patches and tickets, and a community wiki. The post marks an early milestone in Rails' growing identity as an open source project.
key_points: The Rails logo was community-commissioned and designed by John Hicks, reflecting early community investment in the project's identity
The new site unified several Rails properties: manuals, issue tracking (Trac), and community wiki (Instiki)
Rails was already building infrastructure typical of mature open source projects despite being very young
money_quotes: I think it was worth the wait to hold it back until the long-awaited relaunch of the Ruby on Rails website could happen.
The new website brings together all many web properties revolving around Rails.
In short, it's all there. And we're mighty proud to finally be able to share it with the world!
key_insight: The 2004 Rails site relaunch marked an early step in professionalizing the project's public identity and consolidating its growing ecosystem of community tools.
